description: Kernel Architecture & WasmVM Integration — restructure secure-exec into a kernel-based OS with pluggable runtime drivers and migrate WasmVM into the monorepo

packages/kernel/src/vfs.ts defines VirtualFileSystem interface with: readFile, writeFile, mkdir, readdir, stat, unlink, rename, rmdir, realpath, exists, symlink, readlink, lstat, link, chmod, chown, utimes, truncate
VirtualStat extended with isSymbolicLink, ino, nlink, uid, gid
packages/kernel/src/index.ts exports all public types

userStories.title: Implement kernel FD table with shared FileDescriptions

acceptanceCriteria: packages/kernel/src/fd-table.ts implements ProcessFDTable with open, openWith, get, close, dup, dup2, stat, fork, closeAll
FDTableManager creates per-PID tables with stdio FDs (0, 1, 2) pre-allocated
FDTableManager supports fork (copies parent FDs), get, and remove
Shared FileDescription objects have refcounting — closing one FD does not affect duplicates
Cursor position is shared across dup'd FDs
Tests in packages/kernel/test/fd-table.test.ts pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in commit b7a78c4; 8 tests passing

Handles /dev/stdin, /dev/stdout, /dev/stderr

Non-device paths pass through to backing VFS unchanged

drainBuffer reads multiple chunks up to requested length

vitest.config.ts or vitest config in package.json configured for the package

userStories.title: Create kernel integration test infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need test helpers and a MockRuntimeDriver so that kernel integration tests (and later cross-runtime tests) have a reusable foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/kernel/test/helpers.ts exports MockRuntimeDriver implementing RuntimeDriver: name 'mock', configurable commands list, init() stores KernelInterface, spawn() returns a DriverProcess that resolves with configurable exit code and writes configurable stdout/stderr bytes, dispose() is a no-op, kill() immediately resolves

packages/kernel/test/helpers.ts exports createTestKernel(options?: { drivers?: RuntimeDriver[] }) that creates a kernel with TestFileSystem, optionally mounts provided drivers, returns { kernel, vfs }

packages/kernel/test/kernel-integration.test.ts tests kernel+MockRuntimeDriver: mount registers mock commands, kernel.spawn('mock-cmd', []) returns ManagedProcess with correct PID and exit code, kernel.exec('mock-cmd') returns ExecResult with stdout/stderr, exec of unknown command returns non-zero exit code, dispose tears down cleanly

FIX: kernel.ts exec() must wire onStdout/onStderr callbacks BEFORE calling driver.spawn() (or buffer data emitted during spawn). Currently callbacks are attached after spawn() returns, so data emitted synchronously during spawn is lost. Test: MockRuntimeDriver that emits stdout during spawn(), verify exec() captures it

FIX: kernel.ts spawnInternal() must call processTable.register() FIRST to get the real PID, then pass that PID to driver.spawn(). Currently it reads the private nextPid field before register(), creating a race where concurrent spawns get wrong PIDs. Test: spawn 10 processes concurrently, verify all PIDs are unique and match between ProcessContext and process table

FIX: kernel.ts fdRead() must actually read from VFS instead of returning empty bytes. Currently returns new Uint8Array(0) with a TODO comment. Implement: look up FileDescription from FD table, read from VFS at cursor position, advance cursor. Test: open a file via fdOpen, write 'hello' via VFS, fdRead returns 'hello'
packages/kernel/test/kernel-integration.test.ts has 10+ tests passing (6 basic + 4 for the fixes above)
packages/kernel/src/index.ts exports createKernel and all types needed by downstream test files
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Fixed three kernel bugs (stdout callback race, PID allocation race, fdRead empty bytes), added MockRuntimeDriver + createTestKernel helpers, 12 integration tests passing.

userStories.title: Create WasmVM RuntimeDriver
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a createWasmVmRuntime() factory that returns a RuntimeDriver so WasmVM can mount into the kernel.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/runtime/wasmvm/src/driver.ts exports createWasmVmRuntime() returning a RuntimeDriver
RuntimeDriver.name is 'wasmvm'
RuntimeDriver.commands lists all 90+ commands (sh, bash, grep, sed, awk, cat, ls, etc.)
RuntimeDriver.init(kernel) stores KernelInterface reference for syscalls
RuntimeDriver.spawn(command, args, ctx) creates a Worker, loads WASM binary, runs command, returns DriverProcess with pid, exitCode promise, stdin/stdout/stderr write/onData, and kill
RuntimeDriver.dispose() terminates all active workers

CRITICAL: WasmVM's WASI proc_spawn must route through KernelInterface.spawn() so brush-shell pipeline stages can dispatch to any runtime (e.g., 'echo hello | node -e ...' where brush-shell spawns 'node' via the kernel, not internally). Without this, cross-runtime pipelines and npm script execution are impossible
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: proc_spawn routing is the key architectural change: when brush-shell parses a pipeline, each stage calls proc_spawn which goes through the WASI polyfill → KernelInterface.spawn() → kernel command registry → correct runtime driver. This is what enables 'npm run greet' to work (npm spawns sh, sh spawns echo).

userStories.title: Create Node RuntimeDriver
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a createNodeRuntime() factory that wraps the existing Node execution driver behind the RuntimeDriver interface so Node can mount into the kernel.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/runtime/node/src/driver.ts exports createNodeRuntime(options) returning a RuntimeDriver
RuntimeDriver.name is 'node'
RuntimeDriver.commands lists ['node', 'npm', 'npx']
RuntimeDriver.init(kernel) stores KernelInterface, configures bridge to use kernel VFS/FD/process
RuntimeDriver.spawn(command, args, ctx) creates an isolate, runs code, returns DriverProcess
RuntimeDriver.dispose() disposes all active isolates

CRITICAL: The bridge child_process.spawn/exec must be backed by KernelInterface.spawn() instead of the host CommandExecutor. Create a CommandExecutor adapter that wraps KernelInterface.spawn() and returns SpawnedProcess wrapping ManagedProcess. This is what makes 'npm run greet' → child_process.spawn('sh', ['-c', 'echo hello']) route through the kernel to WasmVM

CRITICAL: spawn('npm', args) must locate npm's entry script. The Node RuntimeDriver must resolve 'npm'/'npx' by finding the entry point (e.g., node_modules/.bin/npm or the host Node installation's npm) and executing it in a V8 isolate. Bridge fs reads from kernel VFS for project files but may need ModuleAccessFileSystem overlay for npm's own modules
Existing secure-exec Node tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in commit 0f5140b. KernelCommandExecutor adapter wraps kernel.spawn() as CommandExecutor for child_process bridge routing. npm/npx resolved from host Node installation. Kernel spawnManaged fixed to forward onStdout/onStderr from spawn options. 20 tests covering factory, lifecycle, kernel integration, command routing, and exploit paths.

userStories.title: Create Python RuntimeDriver
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a createPythonRuntime() factory that wraps the existing Pyodide driver behind the RuntimeDriver interface so Python can mount into the kernel.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/runtime/python/src/driver.ts exports createPythonRuntime(options) returning a RuntimeDriver
RuntimeDriver.name is 'python'
RuntimeDriver.commands lists ['python', 'python3', 'pip']
RuntimeDriver.init(kernel) stores KernelInterface, configures Pyodide worker to use kernel VFS
RuntimeDriver.spawn(command, args, ctx) creates a Pyodide worker, runs code, returns DriverProcess
RuntimeDriver.dispose() terminates Pyodide worker

Pyodide worker exposes a 'kernelSpawn' RPC method. Python's os.system() is monkey-patched to call kernelSpawn('sh', ['-c', command]) via the host RPC bridge, which calls KernelInterface.spawn(). Python's subprocess.run/Popen are monkey-patched similarly. Without this, Python cannot spawn shell commands through the kernel
Existing secure-exec Python tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed: createPythonRuntime() factory at packages/runtime/python/src/driver.ts. Single shared Pyodide worker with kernelSpawn RPC. os.system/subprocess monkey-patched to route through kernel.spawn(). 22 tests pass, existing Python tests pass.

userStories.title: Create cross-runtime integration test suite and wire kernel.exec() end-to-end
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a cross-runtime integration test suite in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/ and kernel.exec() working end-to-end with real runtime drivers.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/helpers.ts exports createIntegrationKernel(options?: { runtimes?: ('wasmvm' | 'node' | 'python')[] }) that creates a kernel with InMemoryFileSystem from @secure-exec/os-browser, mounts requested real runtime drivers (createWasmVmRuntime, createNodeRuntime, createPythonRuntime), returns { kernel, vfs, dispose }

packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/helpers.ts exports conditional skip helpers: skipUnlessWasmBuilt() checks wasmvm/target/wasm32-wasip1/release/multicall.wasm exists, skipUnlessPyodide() checks pyodide availability

packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/exec-integration.test.ts tests kernel.exec() with real WasmVM driver: exec('echo hello') returns { code: 0, stdout: 'hello\n' }, exec('ls /tmp') lists files, exec('cat /tmp/test.txt') reads file contents, exec('nonexistent-command') returns non-zero code
packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/exec-integration.test.ts tests kernel.spawn() with real WasmVM: spawn returns ManagedProcess, stdout onData fires, exitCode resolves
Each test creates a fresh kernel via createIntegrationKernel() — no shared state between tests

CRITICAL: createIntegrationKernel mounts drivers in fixed order: WasmVM first (provides sh/bash/coreutils), then Node (overrides WasmVM's 'node' stub with real V8), then Python (overrides WasmVM's 'python' stub with real Pyodide). Mount order determines command precedence — last-mounted wins. Document this in a code comment
8+ integration tests passing (skipped gracefully if WASM binary not built)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed: helpers.ts exports createIntegrationKernel (mounts WasmVM→Node→Python in fixed order), skipUnlessWasmBuilt, skipUnlessPyodide. exec-integration.test.ts has 12 tests (8 exec + 4 spawn) covering echo, ls, cat, env, pipes, redirection, stderr, nonexistent commands, spawn PID/stdout/exitCode, kernel isolation. Uses relative imports to avoid cyclic deps (runtime-node depends on secure-exec). All tests skip gracefully when WASM binary not built.

userStories.title: Route Node bridge child_process.spawn through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Node bridge's child_process.spawn polyfill to route through the kernel's command registry instead of the host's child_process, enabling cross-runtime command execution from Node code.
acceptanceCriteria: Node bridge child_process.spawn('ls', ['/tmp']) resolves via kernel command registry to WasmVM runtime
Node bridge child_process.execSync('echo hello') works through kernel
Spawned processes get proper PIDs from kernel process table
stdout/stderr from spawned processes are piped back to the Node caller
Commands not in the registry return ENOENT-like error

Tests in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/bridge-child-process.test.ts: create kernel with WasmVM + Node mounted via createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'] }), run Node code that calls require('child_process').execSync('echo hello'), verify output
Existing secure-exec child_process tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Fixed KernelInterface.spawn to forward onStdout/onStderr callbacks. Integration tests at packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/bridge-child-process.test.ts.

userStories.title: Implement cross-runtime pipes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need cross-runtime pipelines to work so commands like 'echo hello | node -e "process.stdin.pipe(process.stdout)"' execute correctly across WasmVM and Node runtimes.
acceptanceCriteria: kernel.exec('echo hello | cat') works (WasmVM-to-WasmVM pipe via kernel PipeManager)
kernel.exec('echo hello | node -e "..."') pipes WasmVM stdout to Node stdin when both runtimes mounted
kernel.exec('cat file | python -c "..."') pipes WasmVM stdout to Python stdin when both runtimes mounted
Pipe EOF propagation: when writer closes, reader gets EOF
PipeManager createPipeFDs allocates FDs in the kernel FD table for both ends

PREREQUISITE: kernel SpawnOptions must support FD overrides so a parent process can say 'spawn this child with FD 0 = pipe read-end, FD 1 = pipe write-end'. Add stdinFd/stdoutFd/stderrFd fields to SpawnOptions. spawnInternal uses these instead of default /dev/stdin,stdout,stderr when provided

PREREQUISITE: brush-shell must use KernelInterface.pipe() to create kernel pipes before spawning pipeline stages, then pass pipe FDs to KernelInterface.spawn() via SpawnOptions. This is the mechanism that connects echo's stdout to node's stdin

Tests in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/cross-runtime-pipes.test.ts: use createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'] }), test WasmVM|WasmVM pipe, WasmVM|Node pipe, EOF propagation. Separate describe block for Python pipes using createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'python'] }) with skipUnlessPyodide()
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented: (1) SpawnOptions stdinFd/stdoutFd/stderrFd fields, (2) spawnInternal creates child FD table with pipe descriptions from caller's table, (3) fdRead routes pipes through PipeManager.read(), (4) KernelInterface.pipe(pid) installs FDs, (5) WasmVM proc_spawn/fd_pipe/proc_kill host imports match Rust FFI, (6) stdout writer redirected through kernel pipe when FD is overridden. Tests at packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/cross-runtime-pipes.test.ts.

userStories.title: Harden runtime driver tests — remove escape hatches and fix fake assertions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the existing runtime driver tests to have real assertions so they actually catch regressions instead of silently passing when features don't work.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/runtime/node/test/driver.test.ts 'child_process.spawn routes through kernel to other drivers': remove the `if (code === 0)` guard — assert unconditionally that code === 0 AND output contains 'mock-echo-output'. If child_process routing is broken, this test must FAIL not silently pass

packages/runtime/node/test/driver.test.ts 'cannot access host filesystem directly': replace `expect(stderr).not.toContain('SECURITY_BREACH')` with positive assertions — expect the catch block to execute (stderr contains 'blocked:'), verify stdout does NOT contain real /etc/passwd content like 'root:x:0:0'

packages/runtime/node/test/driver.test.ts 'cannot spawn unlimited processes via fork bomb': replace the placeholder `console.log('safe')` with a real test that spawns 5+ child processes from within Node via child_process.exec, verifies each gets a unique PID from the kernel process table, and all complete

packages/runtime/wasmvm/test/driver.test.ts: add new describe.skipIf(!hasWasmBinary) block 'real execution' with tests: exec('echo hello') stdout is 'hello\n', exec('cat /dev/null') exits 0, exec('false') exits non-zero — tests actual WASM execution not just stub behavior with nonexistent binary
No test may pass when the feature it tests is broken — every assertion must be unconditional
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Fixed all three escape hatch categories: (1) removed if(code===0) guard on execSync routing test — now unconditionally asserts code===0 and output contains mock-echo-output, (2) replaced negative SECURITY_BREACH assertion with positive stderr.toContain('blocked:') and stdout.not.toContain('root:x:0:0'), (3) replaced placeholder fork bomb test with real 5-child-process spawning via execSync. Also added WasmVM real execution tests (echo hello, cat /dev/null, false). Fixed root cause: NodeRuntimeDriver was calling createNodeDriver without permissions, causing EACCES on child_process.spawn — added allowAllChildProcess.

userStories.title: Test stdin streaming (writeStdin/closeStdin) through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests proving that writeStdin delivers data to spawned processes and closeStdin triggers EOF, since this critical path has zero test coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/kernel/test/kernel-integration.test.ts: new describe block 'stdin streaming' with MockRuntimeDriver. Test: spawn mock process, call proc.writeStdin(new TextEncoder().encode('test data')), verify MockRuntimeDriver's DriverProcess.writeStdin received the bytes

packages/runtime/node/test/driver.test.ts: new test 'writeStdin delivers data to Node process'. Spawn node -e that reads all stdin then echoes it: `let d='';process.stdin.on('data',c=>d+=c);process.stdin.on('end',()=>console.log(d.trim()))`. Call proc.writeStdin(encode('hello from stdin')), proc.closeStdin(). Verify stdout contains 'hello from stdin'

packages/runtime/node/test/driver.test.ts: new test 'closeStdin without write triggers empty EOF'. Spawn node -e that counts stdin bytes. Call closeStdin() immediately. Verify process exits 0 and reports 0 bytes

packages/runtime/wasmvm/test/driver.test.ts: new test (skipIf no binary) 'writeStdin to cat'. Spawn 'cat' via kernel, proc.writeStdin(encode('stdin-data\n')), proc.closeStdin(). Verify stdout contains 'stdin-data'
All stdin tests use unconditional assertions — no escape hatches
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented writeStdin/closeStdin for all three drivers. Kernel test verifies MockRuntimeDriver receives bytes. Node driver buffers stdin data and passes to exec() as stdin option. WasmVM driver uses kernel pipe (pipe+dup2+fdWrite) routed to worker via stdinReader. All tests use unconditional assertions.

userStories.title: Test cross-runtime VFS consistency — writes visible across runtimes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests proving that file writes in one runtime are immediately visible to reads in another runtime, since all runtimes share the kernel VFS.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/vfs-consistency.test.ts using createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'] })
Test 'kernel write visible to Node': kernel.writeFile('/tmp/test.txt', 'hello'), then exec node -e fs.readFileSync('/tmp/test.txt','utf8') — stdout contains 'hello'
Test 'Node write visible to WasmVM': exec node -e fs.writeFileSync('/tmp/node-wrote.txt','from-node'), then exec 'cat /tmp/node-wrote.txt' — stdout contains 'from-node'
Test 'Node write visible to kernel API': exec node -e fs.writeFileSync('/tmp/k.txt','data'), then kernel.readFile('/tmp/k.txt') returns 'data'
Test 'directory listing consistent across runtimes': kernel.writeFile creates 3 files in /tmp, exec 'ls /tmp' (WasmVM) and exec node -e readdirSync('/tmp') both list the same file names
Test 'ENOENT consistent across runtimes': exec 'cat /nonexistent' exits non-zero AND exec node -e readFileSync('/nonexistent') exits non-zero — both error on missing files
Gracefully skipped if WASM binary not built
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: 5 tests covering: kernel write→Node read, Node write→WasmVM cat, Node write→kernel readTextFile, directory listing consistency, ENOENT consistency. All gracefully skipped when WASM binary not built.

userStories.title: Test kernel dispose with active processes across runtimes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests proving that kernel.dispose() terminates all running processes and cleans up resources even when processes are actively running in multiple runtimes.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/kernel/test/kernel-integration.test.ts: new describe block 'dispose with active processes'. Test: spawn 5 MockRuntimeDriver processes that never resolve their exit promise, call kernel.dispose(), verify all DriverProcess.kill() was called, verify dispose() resolves within 5s
packages/kernel/test/kernel-integration.test.ts: test 'spawn after dispose throws disposed'. Call dispose(), then attempt kernel.spawn() — verify it throws error containing 'disposed'
packages/kernel/test/kernel-integration.test.ts: test 'exec after dispose rejects with disposed'. Call dispose(), then attempt kernel.exec() — verify it rejects with error containing 'disposed'
New test file packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/dispose-behavior.test.ts using createIntegrationKernel
Integration test: spawn WasmVM 'sleep 60', call dispose(), verify resolves within 5s timeout
Integration test: spawn Node `setTimeout(()=>{},60000)`, call dispose(), verify resolves within 5s timeout
Integration test: spawn processes in BOTH WasmVM and Node simultaneously, dispose, verify both terminated and dispose resolves
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: 3 kernel unit tests (5-process kill, spawn-after-dispose, exec-after-dispose) + 3 integration tests (WasmVM sleep, Node setTimeout, both simultaneously). Integration tests gracefully skipped when WASM binary not built.

userStories.title: Test cross-runtime error and exit code propagation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests proving that exit codes and stderr flow correctly across runtime boundaries when processes fail, including through nested cross-runtime spawns.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/error-propagation.test.ts using createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'] })
Test 'Node non-zero exit propagates': kernel.exec('node -e "process.exit(42)"') returns exitCode 42
Test 'WasmVM non-zero exit propagates': kernel.exec('sh -c "exit 7"') returns exitCode 7
Test 'Node stderr captured by kernel': kernel.exec('node -e "console.error(\"err-msg\")"') returns stderr containing 'err-msg'
Test 'WasmVM stderr captured by kernel': kernel.exec('echo error >&2') returns stderr containing 'error'

Test 'nested cross-runtime exit code propagation': kernel.exec('sh -c "node -e \"process.exit(42)\""') — WasmVM shell spawns Node via kernel, Node exits 42, shell propagates exit code. This tests exit code flowing through TWO runtime boundary crossings: kernel→WasmVM→kernel→Node→exit(42)→kernel→WasmVM→kernel
Test 'Node captures WasmVM child failure': exec Node code that runs child_process.execSync('sh -c "exit 3"') in a try/catch and logs the exit code — verify stdout contains '3'
Gracefully skipped if WASM binary not built
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: 6 tests: Node exit code, WasmVM exit code, Node stderr, WasmVM stderr, nested cross-runtime exit propagation (kernel→WasmVM→kernel→Node→exit(42)), Node captures WasmVM child failure via execSync. All gracefully skipped when WASM binary not built.

userStories.title: Test module resolution through kernel VFS
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests proving that Node's require() resolves modules through the kernel VFS so Node code can load project files, not just built-in modules.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/module-resolution.test.ts using createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'] })

Test 'require relative CJS module': write /app/lib/utils.js to VFS with `module.exports.add = (a,b) => a+b`, write /app/index.js that does `console.log(require('./lib/utils').add(1,2))`. Exec 'node /app/index.js' — stdout contains '3'

Test 'require from node_modules': write /app/node_modules/my-pkg/index.js to VFS with `module.exports.greet = () => 'hello'`, write /app/main.js that does `console.log(require('my-pkg').greet())`. Exec 'node /app/main.js' — stdout contains 'hello'
Test 'require missing module gives MODULE_NOT_FOUND': exec `node -e "require('./nonexistent')"` with cwd /app — exits non-zero, stderr contains 'Cannot find module' or 'MODULE_NOT_FOUND'

Test 'require nested relative path': write /app/src/handlers/index.js that does `console.log(require('../../lib/utils').add(3,4))` (reusing /app/lib/utils.js from above). Exec 'node /app/src/handlers/index.js' — stdout contains '7'
Test 'require JSON file': write /app/config.json to VFS with `{"port": 3000}`, exec `node -e "console.log(require('./config.json').port)"` with cwd /app — stdout contains '3000'
Gracefully skipped if WASM binary not built
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: 5 tests: require relative CJS, require from node_modules, require missing module (MODULE_NOT_FOUND), require nested relative path, require JSON file. All gracefully skipped when WASM binary not built.

userStories.title: Implement FD inheritance for child processes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need child processes to inherit their parent's file descriptor table so redirections and pipes work correctly across process boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: When kernel.spawn forks a process, child gets a copy of parent's FD table via FDTableManager.fork
Inherited FDs share cursor position with parent (via shared FileDescription refcounting)
Child can close inherited FDs without affecting parent
Redirections (e.g., '> /tmp/out.txt') modify child's FD table before exec

Unit tests in packages/kernel/test/kernel-integration.test.ts using MockRuntimeDriver: spawn with parent FD table, verify child inherits FDs, verify closing child FD doesn't affect parent, verify shared cursor position
Integration test in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/fd-inheritance.test.ts using createIntegrationKernel: exec('echo hello > /tmp/out.txt') writes to file, exec('cat < /tmp/in.txt') reads from file
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: FD inheritance via fork + refcount-aware pipe close

userStories.title: Implement signal forwarding across runtimes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need kill(pid, signal) to work across runtimes so processes can be terminated regardless of which runtime owns them.
acceptanceCriteria: kernel process table kill(pid, SIGTERM) routes to the correct runtime driver's DriverProcess.kill()
SIGTERM gives the process a chance to clean up before termination
SIGKILL immediately terminates the process
kill() on a non-existent PID returns ESRCH error
Signal delivery works across runtimes (Node process can kill a WasmVM process and vice versa)

Unit tests in packages/kernel/test/kernel-integration.test.ts using MockRuntimeDriver: spawn process, send SIGTERM, verify kill() called on DriverProcess, verify process exits, verify ESRCH for bad PID

Integration test in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/signal-forwarding.test.ts using createIntegrationKernel: spawn a long-running WasmVM process (e.g., 'sleep 60'), send SIGTERM via kernel, verify it exits with non-zero code
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Signal routing via process table, ManagedProcess.kill() routes through processTable.kill() for status checks

userStories.title: E2e: run existing project-matrix fixtures through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the existing project-matrix fixture projects (semver-pass, dotenv-pass, module-access-pass, crypto-random-pass, fs-metadata-rename-pass, rivetkit, net-unsupported-fail) to run through the kernel with output parity against host Node.
acceptanceCriteria: Test in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/e2e-project-matrix.test.ts
Test discovers fixture projects from packages/secure-exec/tests/projects/ by reading fixture.json metadata (entry, expectation, fail config) — reuse the same discovery logic as project-matrix.test.ts

For each fixture: prepare project (npm install in temp dir, cache by content hash — reuse prepareFixtureProject pattern from project-matrix.test.ts), run entry via host Node, run entry via kernel (createIntegrationKernel with WasmVM + Node mounted, NodeFileSystem pointed at prepared project dir)
For 'pass' fixtures: kernel output (code, stdout, stderr) matches host Node output
For 'fail' fixtures: kernel returns expected non-zero code and stderr includes expected string
Fixture semver-pass: require('semver'), validate/satisfies/compare — tests CJS package resolution
Fixture dotenv-pass: require('dotenv'), loads .env — tests filesystem + CJS
Fixture module-access-pass: require local vendor packages — tests file: dependency resolution
ESM test: add a new fixture esm-import-pass with type:module package.json that does import { Hono } from 'hono' and logs typeof app.fetch — tests ESM module resolution through kernel
Gracefully skipped if WASM binary not built
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Reuse infrastructure from packages/secure-exec/tests/project-matrix.test.ts: discoverFixtures(), prepareFixtureProject(), runHostExecution(). The kernel side replaces runSandboxExecution() with kernel.exec('node <entry>'). Add one new ESM fixture to cover import syntax. KEY DECISION: use NodeFileSystem pointed at the prepared project dir (which has node_modules from host npm install) so that require() resolves naturally. The kernel VFS root IS the project dir, cwd is '/'. ModuleAccessFileSystem overlay is NOT needed when using NodeFileSystem because node_modules is already on disk. InMemoryFileSystem would require copying hundreds of MB — impractical.

userStories.title: E2e: npm run scripts execute shell commands through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that 'npm run <script>' works through the kernel, where npm reads package.json, finds the script, and executes it via the shell (routed through kernel command registry to WasmVM).
acceptanceCriteria: Test in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/e2e-npm-scripts.test.ts

Test setup: createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'] }), write package.json with scripts: { 'greet': 'echo hello world', 'count': 'echo one && echo two && echo three', 'env-check': 'echo $npm_package_name' }
kernel.exec('npm run greet') stdout includes 'hello world'
kernel.exec('npm run count') stdout includes 'one', 'two', 'three' — tests sequential shell commands (&&) through WasmVM
kernel.exec('npm run env-check') stdout includes the package name — tests npm env variable injection through shell
kernel.exec('npm run nonexistent') returns non-zero exit code
Each test creates a fresh kernel
Gracefully skipped if WASM binary not built
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is the key cross-runtime integration test: Node (npm) reads package.json → spawns shell via child_process (routed through kernel to WasmVM) → WasmVM executes the shell command → output flows back through kernel pipes to npm → captured by kernel.exec(). Tests the full Node→kernel→WasmVM→kernel→Node round-trip.

userStories.title: E2e: npm install a package through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that 'npm install' works through the kernel with network access, proving the full package installation flow (download, extract, write to VFS) works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Test in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/e2e-npm-install.test.ts

Test setup: createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'], permissions: { ...allowAllFs, ...allowAllNetwork } }), write minimal package.json with { 'dependencies': { 'left-pad': '1.3.0' } } to VFS
kernel.exec('npm install') completes with exit code 0
After install, node_modules/left-pad/ exists in the kernel VFS
kernel.exec('node -e "console.log(require('left-pad')('hi', 10))"') outputs '        hi' — proves installed package is usable
Test timeout set to 30s (network-dependent)
Gracefully skipped if WASM binary not built or if no network available
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Uses left-pad (tiny, no native deps) to keep install fast. KEY ISSUES: (1) Network: The kernel has no network layer. The Node RuntimeDriver must pass through network requests to the host runtime (Node.js native fetch/http). The kernel only provides the permission check via NetworkAccessRequest. Add a checkNetworkPermission() method to KernelInterface if not present. (2) Filesystem: Use NodeFileSystem so npm itself is available from the host Node installation. The project dir (with package.json) should be a temp dir on the host filesystem. npm writes node_modules into this real dir via the kernel VFS. (3) npm resolution: npm is a Node.js program installed with the host Node. The Node RuntimeDriver must locate npm's entry script from the host system when spawn('npm', args) is called.

userStories.title: E2e: npx execution and pipe-based Node eval through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need npx to work through the kernel and WasmVM-to-Node pipes to work for 'echo ... | node -e ...' patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Test in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/e2e-npx-and-pipes.test.ts

npx test: createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'] }), kernel.exec('npx -y cowsay hello') or kernel.exec('npx semver 1.2.3') outputs expected result — tests npx binary resolution through kernel command registry
Pipe test: kernel.exec('echo 1+2 | node -e "process.stdin.on('data', d => console.log(eval(d.toString().trim())))"') outputs '3' — tests WasmVM stdout piped to Node stdin

Pipe test: kernel.exec('echo hello world | node -e "let d=\'\'; process.stdin.on('data',c=>d+=c); process.stdin.on('end',()=>console.log(d.trim().toUpperCase()))"') outputs 'HELLO WORLD' — tests pipe with end event
Pipe test: kernel.exec('cat /tmp/data.txt | node -e "..."') where /tmp/data.txt is pre-written to VFS — tests file→pipe→Node
Each test creates a fresh kernel
Gracefully skipped if WASM binary not built
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: npx tests verify the npm/npx command resolution chain through the kernel. Pipe tests exercise the WasmVM→kernel PipeManager→Node stdin path, complementing the unit-level pipe tests in US-022.

userStories.title: E2e: npm postinstall lifecycle scripts through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need npm postinstall lifecycle scripts to execute through the kernel, proving that npm's child_process.spawn for lifecycle hooks routes through the kernel command registry to WasmVM.
acceptanceCriteria: Test in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/e2e-npm-lifecycle.test.ts
Test setup: createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'], permissions: { ...allowAllFs, ...allowAllNetwork } })
Create a fixture with package.json containing: scripts: { 'postinstall': 'echo POSTINSTALL_RAN > /tmp/marker.txt' }, dependencies: { 'left-pad': '1.3.0' }
kernel.exec('npm install') completes with exit code 0
After install, /tmp/marker.txt exists in kernel VFS and contains 'POSTINSTALL_RAN' — proves postinstall script ran through WasmVM shell
Also test preinstall: scripts: { 'preinstall': 'echo PREINSTALL_RAN > /tmp/pre-marker.txt' } — verify pre-marker.txt exists after npm install
Gracefully skipped if WASM binary not built or if no network available
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Lifecycle scripts are the trickiest npm integration: npm spawns a shell to run each lifecycle script, that shell must route through the kernel to WasmVM. Tests: Node (npm) → child_process.spawn('sh', ['-c', 'echo ...']) → kernel command registry → WasmVM shell → writes to kernel VFS.

userStories.title: E2e: concurrently package runs parallel processes through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the concurrently package to work through the kernel, testing that multiple concurrent child processes spawned via Node's child_process all route correctly through the kernel to WasmVM.
acceptanceCriteria: Test in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/e2e-concurrently.test.ts
Test setup: createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'] }), pre-install concurrently in project node_modules (use host npm install + NodeFileSystem overlay)
kernel.exec('npx concurrently "echo hello" "echo world"') stdout contains both 'hello' and 'world'
kernel.exec('npx concurrently "echo one && echo two" "echo three"') stdout contains 'one', 'two', 'three' — tests multiple processes with shell operators
kernel.exec('npx concurrently --kill-others-on-fail "echo success" "sh -c exit 1"') returns non-zero exit code — tests process group failure handling
Verify kernel process table handles concurrent PID allocation without conflicts
Gracefully skipped if WASM binary not built
Test timeout set to 30s
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: concurrently spawns multiple child_process instances simultaneously. This tests: kernel process table concurrent PID allocation, kernel command registry handling multiple simultaneous resolves, WasmVM running multiple workers in parallel, kernel pipe multiplexing stdout from multiple processes.

userStories.title: E2e: Next.js build runs through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need 'next build' to complete successfully through the kernel on a minimal Next.js project, proving the kernel can handle a complex real-world build pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Test in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/e2e-nextjs-build.test.ts

Test setup: createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'node'], permissions: { ...allowAllFs, ...allowAllEnv } }), prepare a minimal Next.js project on host with: package.json (next, react, react-dom), next.config.js (output: 'export' for static build), app/page.tsx (simple 'Hello World' component), app/layout.tsx (minimal root layout)
Host-side npm install to populate node_modules, then mount via NodeFileSystem
kernel.exec('npx next build') completes with exit code 0
After build, .next/ directory or out/ directory (for export mode) exists in kernel VFS with build output
Test timeout set to 120s (Next.js build is slow)
Gracefully skipped if WASM binary not built
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: KNOWN BLOCKERS: (1) SWC is a .node native addon — ModuleAccessFileSystem blocks .node files (ERR_MODULE_ACCESS_NATIVE_ADDON). Must set NEXT_DISABLE_SWC=1 to force Babel fallback or configure the kernel to allow native addons. (2) Next.js uses worker_threads for parallel compilation — not supported in V8 isolate sandbox. May need NEXT_EXPERIMENTAL_WORKERS=0 or similar config. (3) The build is slow with Babel (minutes). Ralph should attempt the build, document failure modes, and apply workarounds (disable SWC, single-threaded mode, output:'export' for static). If the build cannot complete due to sandbox limitations, document what fails and mark the story with the specific blockers for future resolution.

userStories.title: E2e: Python + WasmVM basic integration through kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need basic Python code execution through the kernel and cross-runtime pipes between Python and WasmVM to work, proving the Python RuntimeDriver integrates with the kernel.
acceptanceCriteria: Test in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/e2e-python-wasmvm.test.ts
Test setup: createIntegrationKernel({ runtimes: ['wasmvm', 'python'] })
kernel.exec('python -c "print(42)"') returns { code: 0, stdout: '42\n' } — basic Python execution
kernel.exec('python -c "import json; print(json.dumps({\"ok\": True}))"') returns { code: 0, stdout containing '{"ok": true}' } — tests Python stdlib import
kernel.exec('echo hello | python -c "import sys; print(sys.stdin.read().strip().upper())"') returns { code: 0, stdout: 'HELLO\n' } — tests WasmVM-to-Python pipe
kernel.exec('python -c "import os; os.system(\"echo from-shell\")"') stdout includes 'from-shell' — tests Python spawning shell through kernel command registry to WasmVM
kernel.exec('python -c "import sys; sys.exit(42)"') returns { code: 42 } — tests exit code propagation
Each test creates a fresh kernel
Gracefully skipped via skipUnlessPyodide()
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Tests the Python RuntimeDriver + kernel integration. DEPENDENCY: os.system test depends on the kernelSpawn RPC and os.system monkey-patch added in US-019 (Python RuntimeDriver). Without that, os.system raises ENOSYS because Pyodide/WASI does not support libc system(). The WasmVM-to-Python pipe test depends on US-022 (SpawnOptions FD overrides) being complete. skipUnlessPyodide() checks for Pyodide availability since it requires downloading the runtime.

userStories.title: Create kernel behavioral contracts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need behavioral contracts for the kernel package so future changes are reviewed against explicit requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: .agent/contracts/kernel.md exists defining behavioral requirements for: VFS interface semantics, FD table open/close/dup/dup2/fork, process table register/waitpid/kill/zombie cleanup, device layer intercepts and EPERM rules, pipe manager blocking read/EOF/drain, command registry resolution and /bin population, permission deny-by-default wrapping

.agent/contracts/kernel-runtime-driver.md exists defining: RuntimeDriver interface contract (init/spawn/dispose lifecycle), command registration rules, ProcessContext requirements, DriverProcess exit/kill/stdio contract
Both contracts use Requirement/Scenario/WHEN/THEN format consistent with existing contracts
.agent/contracts/README.md updated with new contract entries in the index table
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update existing contracts for kernel model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need existing contracts updated to reflect the kernel-mediated execution model so contracts remain the authoritative source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: .agent/contracts/node-runtime.md updated: execution goes through kernel mount + spawn, not standalone driver construction
.agent/contracts/node-bridge.md updated: child_process.spawn routes through kernel command registry
.agent/contracts/node-permissions.md updated: documents interaction between kernel-level permissions (wrapFileSystem) and existing secure-exec permission wrappers

.agent/contracts/runtime-driver-integration-testing.md updated: test infrastructure supports kernel-aware TestContext with mounted drivers, references packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/ for cross-runtime tests

.agent/contracts/runtime-driver-test-suite-structure.md updated: adds kernel test patterns (kernel unit tests with MockRuntimeDriver in packages/kernel/test/, cross-runtime integration tests in packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/)
.agent/contracts/compatibility-governance.md updated: cross-runtime parity requirements (same VFS/FD/process state across WasmVM, Node, Python)
All contract changes are internally consistent and use existing WHEN/THEN format
Typecheck passes
